axis: bridge / alert history trend analyzer — actionable metrics over the FIRED-event log
title: tool/atlas_alert_trend.hexa — read-only trend analyzer over state/atlas_alert_history.tsv
summary: ω-bridge-21 introduced state/atlas_alert_history.tsv as the canonical FIRED-event log, but no tool reasoned over it. This cycle adds tool/atlas_alert_trend.hexa — a sweeper-safe .hexa analyzer that reads the history TSV and emits six trend metrics: alert_count_total, alert_count_last_window (default 24h), per_rule_count, mtba_per_rule (mean time between alerts in seconds), top_rule_in_window, and fire_rate_per_hour (total fires / wall-time-span-hours). Read-only on the TSV (raw 71 / 77). Verdicts: PASS (≥3 rows), INSUFFICIENT (<3 rows; deferred-recommendation pattern from ω-network-3), EMPTY (header only), SKIP (file missing). CLI: --history PATH / --window-hours H / --json / --quiet / --help. Sentinel: __ALERT_TREND__ <verdict> total=N last_window=W top_rule=R fire_rate_per_hour=X. Six fixture cases validated.

predecessors.omega_bridge_17: built tool/atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa — emits __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED sentinels for warn_ratio + insufficient_ratio rules
predecessors.omega_bridge_19: added consecutive_warn (streak) and recent_warn_ratio (time window) rules with their own FIRED shapes
predecessors.omega_bridge_21: extended subscriber to persist FIRED outcomes to state/atlas_alert_history.tsv (TAB-separated, append-only, 7-column schema); force-added the file as schema stub
predecessors.omega_network_3: established the 'deferred recommendation' pattern (INSUFFICIENT verdict) for analyzers that need a minimum sample count before emitting metrics

context.problem: The history TSV accumulates FIRED rows over time but no tool reads it. Without an analyzer, operators cannot answer 'how often is each rule firing?', 'is the alert rate accelerating?', 'when was the last consecutive_warn streak?' — questions that require trend metrics over the event log.

design_alternatives_considered.option: A — embed analyzer logic inside atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa as a new --trend mode
design_alternatives_considered.rejected_because: violates single-responsibility (subscriber is a producer; trend is a consumer); coupling the two means a trend bug can break the subscriber; subscriber is already on its third feature increment (-17, -19, -21)

design_alternatives_considered.option: B — separate sweeper-safe .hexa tool that reads the TSV (CHOSEN)
design_alternatives_considered.chosen_because: matches the bridge_anomaly.hexa precedent (ω-bridge-14): producer/consumer split, trivial to test in isolation, easy to add new metrics later, sentinel surface is independent (__ALERT_TREND__ vs __ATLAS_ALERT__)

design_alternatives_considered.option: C — shell pipeline (awk + sort + uniq -c) wrapped in a .sh
design_alternatives_considered.rejected_because: raw 9 hexa-only-suffix wants new tooling in .hexa; ISO-8601 datetime arithmetic + per-rule MTBA in awk is brittle; the producer is already .hexa so the consumer should match

structural_blocks: header (raw-9, raw-71, raw-77 compliance + sentinel doc + usage examples)
TSV parser: skip # / blank / <6-fields rows; tracks malformed_rows count; tolerates trailing-empty-extra by accepting >=6 fields
per_rule_count + first/last ts per rule (in-hexa loop, no shell-out)
overall first/last ts (lexical compare on ISO-8601)
single python3 shell-out: computes WIN_TOTAL, TOP_RULE, SPAN_HOURS, FIRE_RATE, WIN_RULE per-rule counts in window, MTBA per rule (NA if rule has only 1 fire)
JSON output (when --json): single-line object, includes verdict / per_rule_count / mtba_per_rule (null for rules with <2 fires) / span / fire_rate
sentinel emission (always last): __ALERT_TREND__ <verdict> total=N last_window=W top_rule=R fire_rate_per_hour=X

tsv_columns.name: extra
tsv_columns.note: may be empty — parser accepts 6-field rows when extra omitted by trailing-empty drop

metrics_emitted.name: alert_count_total
metrics_emitted.definition: count of valid (non-comment, well-formed) data rows

metrics_emitted.name: alert_count_last_window
metrics_emitted.definition: rows whose ts falls within [now - window_hours, now]

metrics_emitted.name: per_rule_count
metrics_emitted.definition: histogram of rule firings over file lifetime

metrics_emitted.name: mtba_per_rule
metrics_emitted.definition: mean time between alerts in seconds, per rule (NA if rule has <2 fires)

metrics_emitted.name: top_rule_in_window
metrics_emitted.definition: most-fired rule within window_hours; sentinel value 'none' if window empty; ties broken by first-encountered after sort

metrics_emitted.name: fire_rate_per_hour
metrics_emitted.definition: total / span_hours, where span_hours = (last_ts - first_ts) / 3600; 0.0 if all rows share the same second

flags: --json               emit one-line JSON object before sentinel; suppresses human summary
sentinels: __ALERT_TREND__ INSUFFICIENT total=N last_window=0 top_rule=none fire_rate_per_hour=0.0000  (when total<3)
__ALERT_TREND__ EMPTY        total=0 last_window=0 top_rule=none fire_rate_per_hour=0.0000  (header only)

compliance.raw_9_hexa_only_suffix: new file is tool/atlas_alert_trend.hexa (.hexa suffix; sweeper-safe)
compliance.raw_71_report_only: tool reads the history TSV and parses; never writes; uses /tmp/atlas_alert_trend_*_<ts>_<n>.tsv as scratch (cleaned up post-shell-out)
compliance.raw_73_admissibility: verdict surface mirrors network-3 deferred-recommendation pattern (INSUFFICIENT for sub-quorum data); SKIP for missing file (exit 0); PASS for >=3 rows
compliance.raw_77_append_only: history TSV is consumed read-only — no edits to existing rows, no rewrites; matches the producer contract from ω-bridge-21
compliance.raw_80_sentinel: __ALERT_TREND__ added (PASS / INSUFFICIENT / EMPTY / SKIP shapes); independent of __ATLAS_ALERT__ producer surface
compliance.no_parallel_session_collision: added only tool/atlas_alert_trend.hexa; did NOT touch cli/run.hexa, state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, bisociation/spectra/g_atlas_composite_v3.json, tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa, n6/*, tool/kick_*.hexa, design/kick/*, .own — verified via git status --short before staging
compliance.explicit_path_commit: git add tool/atlas_alert_trend.hexa only — no `git add -A`
compliance.no_anima_commit: closure JSON written to /Users/ghost/core/anima/state/omega_bridge_22_alert_history_trend_analyzer.json — UNTRACKED, no git operations in anima

fixtures.name: fixture_a — typical 10 rows mixed rules over 3-day span
fixtures.expected: PASS verdict, total=10, span≈80h, fire_rate≈0.125, per-rule counts {warn_ratio:5, insufficient_ratio:2, consecutive_warn:2, recent_warn_ratio:1}, MTBA per rule populated for rules with ≥2 fires
fixtures.observed: __ALERT_TREND__ PASS total=10 last_window=2 top_rule=consecutive_warn fire_rate_per_hour=0.1250; rules_seen=4; per_rule_count + MTBA all match expected; recent_warn_ratio MTBA=NA (only 1 fire)

fixtures.name: fixture_b — insufficient (1 row only)
fixtures.expected: INSUFFICIENT verdict, no metric computation, sentinel total=1

fixtures.name: fixture_d — missing path
fixtures.expected: SKIP verdict, exit 0, no metric output

fixtures.name: fixture_e — 5 valid + 2 malformed (1-field stray + 4-field short)
fixtures.observed: warn: malformed row skipped (fields=1) + warn: malformed row skipped (fields=4); __ALERT_TREND__ PASS total=5 last_window=1 top_rule=warn_ratio fire_rate_per_hour=0.0694; malformed=2

fixtures.name: fixture_f — single rule (5 rows all consecutive_warn over 3 days)
fixtures.expected: PASS, top_rule=consecutive_warn, only one entry in per_rule_count, MTBA for consecutive_warn populated, no MTBA entries for other rules
fixtures.observed: __ALERT_TREND__ PASS total=5 last_window=1 top_rule=consecutive_warn fire_rate_per_hour=0.0694; per_rule_count={consecutive_warn:5}; mtba_per_rule={consecutive_warn:64800.0000}

additional_smoke.name: --json mode on fixture_a
additional_smoke.observed: JSON parses via python3 -m json.tool; verdict / total / per_rule_count / mtba_per_rule (with null for rules with <2 fires) all present

additional_smoke.name: --quiet mode on fixture_a
additional_smoke.expected: only the sentinel line on stdout
additional_smoke.observed: single line: __ALERT_TREND__ PASS total=10 last_window=2 top_rule=consecutive_warn fire_rate_per_hour=0.1250

additional_smoke.name: default invocation against live state/atlas_alert_history.tsv (header-only stub from ω-bridge-21)

edge_cases_handled.trailing_empty_extra_field: rows where the extra field is empty (warn_ratio / insufficient_ratio) often serialize without a trailing tab → split yields 6 fields not 7; parser accepts >=6 fields and treats missing 7th as empty
edge_cases_handled.comment_and_blank_lines: lines starting with # and blank lines are skipped before field-count check (no false malformed)
edge_cases_handled.malformed_rows_counted_not_fatal: rows with <6 fields, empty ts, or empty rule increment malformed_rows and are skipped; analyzer continues with the valid rows
edge_cases_handled.single_fire_per_rule_mtba_NA: MTBA requires ≥2 fires for a rule; rules with exactly 1 fire emit MTBA=NA in summary and null in JSON
edge_cases_handled.single_second_span_zero_fire_rate: if first_ts == last_ts (all rows share the same second), span_hours = 0 → fire_rate_per_hour = 0.0 (avoids div-by-zero)
edge_cases_handled.future_dated_rows_excluded_from_window: python computes cutoff = now - window_hours; rows with t > now or t < cutoff are excluded from win_total / top_rule (defensive against clock skew)
edge_cases_handled.iso8601_lexical_compare: first/last ts computed via string < / > since the format %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ is lexically chronological
edge_cases_handled.verdict_stratification: SKIP > EMPTY > INSUFFICIENT > PASS — each early-returns with its own sentinel; no fall-through cases
edge_cases_handled.json_quiet_interaction: --quiet suppresses the human summary; --json overrides --quiet for the JSON line; sentinel emits in all modes (last line of stdout, raw 80)
edge_cases_handled.tmp_file_cleanup: scratch files /tmp/atlas_alert_trend_rows_<ts>_<n>.tsv and _meta_ are rm -f'd after the python shell-out (best-effort, errors suppressed)

operational_notes.default_invocation_today: live state/atlas_alert_history.tsv has 0 data rows (header stub from ω-bridge-21); analyzer emits EMPTY verdict — expected; PASS verdict will surface as the bridge cluster accumulates firings
operational_notes.expected_steady_state: with default cron cadence (~5min) and time-shape rules off-by-default, FIRED rate is typically <1/day; analyzer is cheap to run in any sweep loop without performance concern
operational_notes.window_hours_choice: 24h default chosen to align with daily cron cadence + operator review rhythm; --window-hours 168 gives a weekly view, --window-hours 1 gives near-realtime alarm density
operational_notes.back_compat: no edits to existing tools or files; pure addition; existing __ATLAS_ALERT__ surface unchanged
operational_notes.no_history_growth: tool is read-only; does not contribute to any state file growth

future_candidates: tool/atlas_alert_trend_dashboard.hexa — render trend metrics into a sparkline-style cross-repo dashboard panel
alert escalation policy: feed __ALERT_TREND__ PASS with last_window > N back into atlas_alert_subscriber as a meta-alert (alert about excessive alerting)

rotation hook: when the history TSV span_hours exceeds 24*30, archive to state/atlas_alert_history_<yyyymm>.tsv and rotate the live file (paired with a --history-glob flag in the analyzer to read across rotated files)
rule-pair correlation: extend the analyzer with --correlate to detect rules that fire together within a short coincidence window (e.g. consecutive_warn → recent_warn_ratio within 5min)
wire __ALERT_TREND__ into the ω-network cross-repo dashboard as a sibling panel to __BRIDGE_ANOMALY__
